raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.0,"R1.4":1.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.4","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation centrally concerns reframing the contribution and gap relative to existing research lines.","R1.2":"Positioning against prior work requires checking that the stated prior-work categories logically support the claimed research gap.","R1.4":"Novelty assessment is load-bearing because the request is explicitly about the paper's delta from prior work.","R1.7":"Citation and scholarship are central because prior-work positioning depends on accurate coverage and attribution of the relevant literature.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed where claims such as firstness or absence of prior work exceed what the artifact alone establishes.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is essential because strong novelty language could misrepresent the contribution if not carefully bounded.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is required to keep the projection limited to prior-work positioning rather than rewriting or evaluating the whole artifact.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance applies because inaccurate positioning could mislead readers about the state of the literature.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are required so responsibility remains tied to the delegation and the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation centrally concerns reframing the contribution and gap relative to existing research lines.
rationale.R1.2: Positioning against prior work requires checking that the stated prior-work categories logically support the claimed research gap.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is load-bearing because the request is explicitly about the paper's delta from prior work.
rationale.R1.7: Citation and scholarship are central because prior-work positioning depends on accurate coverage and attribution of the relevant literature.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed where claims such as firstness or absence of prior work exceed what the artifact alone establishes.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is essential because strong novelty language could misrepresent the contribution if not carefully bounded.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is required to keep the projection limited to prior-work positioning rather than rewriting or evaluating the whole artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance applies because inaccurate positioning could mislead readers about the state of the literature.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are required so responsibility remains tied to the delegation and the provided artifact.
